description: Machine-readable experiment casebook derived from the comparative 5+5 discovery pack. All cases are design-only. No contacts have been made. No experiments have been executed.

cases.target_role: Strategic foresight director or federal program-analysis lead
cases.job_to_be_tested: Moving from source material to a decision-ready brief with evidence, review, and governance steps
cases.demo_surface: Console to strategist brief to human-review and forecast-draft handoff
discovery_questions: Where does GAO Center for Strategic Foresight currently lose time or confidence moving from source material to a decision-ready brief?
Which evidence, review, or governance step is mandatory before a forecast or scenario recommendation is trusted?
What proof would you need from a guided pilot before sponsoring a broader evaluation?
Which existing tool, analyst workflow, consultancy, or spreadsheet would this need to complement or replace?
If the workflow helped, what concrete next step would be credible: second review, internal demo, paid pilot, LOI, or no action?
cases.consent_requirement: Explicit verbal consent before recording any notes; public-sector confidentiality boundaries apply
cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent, requests written materials only, or indicates no recurring unmet job

cases.target_organization: UK Government Office for Science Futures and Foresight
cases.target_role: Futures, foresight, or horizon-scanning team lead
cases.job_to_be_tested: Moving from horizon-scanning sources to a decision-ready foresight brief with governance
cases.demo_surface: Actor/countermove brief plus review state and forecast-governance caveats
discovery_questions: Where does UK Government Office for Science Futures and Foresight currently lose time or confidence moving from source material to a decision-ready brief?
Which evidence, review, or governance step is mandatory before a forecast or scenario recommendation is trusted?
What proof would you need from a guided pilot before sponsoring a broader evaluation?
Which existing tool, analyst workflow, consultancy, or spreadsheet would this need to complement or replace?
If the workflow helped, what concrete next step would be credible: second review, internal demo, paid pilot, LOI, or no action?
cases.consent_requirement: Explicit verbal consent; UK government communication standards apply
cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent or indicates no recurring unmet job

cases.consent_requirement: Explicit verbal consent; OECD institutional communication protocols apply
cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent or indicates no recurring unmet job

cases.job_to_be_tested: Producing global foresight and long-range strategy work for complex international issues
cases.demo_surface: Analyst briefing layer plus forecast-draft governance for a Global Foresight scenario
discovery_questions: Where does Atlantic Council GeoStrategy Initiative currently lose time or confidence moving from source material to a decision-ready brief?
Which evidence, review, or governance step is mandatory before a forecast or scenario recommendation is trusted?
What proof would you need from a guided pilot before sponsoring a broader evaluation?
Which existing tool, analyst workflow, consultancy, or spreadsheet would this need to complement or replace?
If the workflow helped, what concrete next step would be credible: second review, internal demo, paid pilot, LOI, or no action?

cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent or indicates no recurring unmet job

cases.target_role: Game theory / political economy professor or teaching faculty
cases.job_to_be_tested: Moving from a game-theory model to a decision-ready brief for students with evidence-linked reasoning
cases.demo_surface: Console to strategist brief with actor reasoning and equilibrium analysis for a classroom case
discovery_questions: How do you currently move from a game-theory model to a decision-ready brief for students?
What evidence or reasoning steps do you require before presenting a case as 'decision-ready'?
Would an evidence-to-actor-reasoning-to-forecast workflow complement your current teaching tools (e.g., MobLab Classroom)?
What would you need to see before adopting this in a course or recommending it to colleagues?
cases.consent_requirement: Explicit verbal consent; no outreach to students; FERPA and Stanford IRB protocols apply if student data is involved. Distinguish quality-improvement from research per HHS OHRP guidance.
cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent, indicates no teaching need, or requests written materials only
cases.moblab_complement_note: This case positions the workflow as a complement to MobLab Classroom (https://www.moblab.com/products/classroom), not a replacement. MobLab provides interactive games, assignments, and gradebook; this workflow provides evidence-to-reasoning governance.

cases.target_organization: MIT OpenCourseWare — Game Theory and Strategic Decision Making
cases.target_role: Course coordinator or instructional designer for strategy courses
cases.job_to_be_tested: Bridging the gap between theory and decision-ready reasoning in online course materials
cases.demo_surface: Console workflow showing evidence bundle, actor map, and equilibrium for a classic game theory scenario
discovery_questions: What tools do students currently use to explore game-theory scenarios interactively?
Where does the gap exist between theory and decision-ready reasoning in your course materials?
Would an evidence-linked actor reasoning workflow complement MobLab or other interactive tools?
What proof would you need before recommending this as a teaching supplement?
cases.consent_requirement: Explicit verbal consent; respect OCW editorial independence; MIT IRB protocols apply if student data is involved
cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent or indicates no need for interactive reasoning tools
cases.moblab_complement_note: This case positions the workflow as a complement to MobLab Classroom, not a replacement.

cases.target_organization: University of Chicago — Pearson Institute for the Study and Resolution of Global Conflicts
cases.target_role: Research fellow or faculty lead in conflict resolution and game theory
cases.job_to_be_tested: Moving from a game-theory model to a policy-relevant recommendation with evidence governance
cases.demo_surface: Console to strategist brief with actor reasoning, countermoves, and forecast-draft governance for a conflict scenario
discovery_questions: How do you currently move from a game-theory model to a policy-relevant recommendation?
What evidence and review steps are required before a forecast is trusted in your research?
Would a governed evidence-to-forecast workflow complement your current analytical tools?
What would convince you to pilot this in a research project or seminar?
cases.consent_requirement: Explicit verbal consent; UChicago IRB protocols apply; no claims about prediction accuracy
cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent or indicates no research need for evidence governance tools
cases.moblab_complement_note: This case positions the workflow as a complement to MobLab Classroom, not a replacement.

cases.target_organization: Yale University — Jackson School of Global Affairs
cases.target_role: Professor of grand strategy, international relations, or decision analysis
cases.job_to_be_tested: Helping students explore the gap between strategic theory and evidence-linked decision-making
cases.demo_surface: Console workflow showing evidence bundle, actor reasoning, and equilibrium for a geopolitical strategy case
discovery_questions: How do students currently explore the gap between strategic theory and evidence-linked decision-making?
What tools do you use to show actor reasoning and countermoves in a policy scenario?
Would a governed evidence-to-forecast workflow complement your current case-teaching approach?
What proof would you need before adopting this as a teaching tool?
cases.consent_requirement: Explicit verbal consent; respect academic independence; Yale IRB protocols apply if student data is involved. Separate quality-improvement from research per HHS OHRP guidance.
cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent or indicates no teaching need
cases.moblab_complement_note: This case positions the workflow as a complement to MobLab Classroom, not a replacement.

cases.target_role: Strategy professor or case-method teaching faculty
cases.job_to_be_tested: Helping students move from case evidence to strategic recommendations with reasoning governance
cases.demo_surface: Console to strategist brief with evidence bundle, actor map, and equilibrium for a business strategy case
discovery_questions: How do you currently help students move from case evidence to strategic recommendations?
What reasoning steps do you require students to show before accepting a recommendation?
Would an evidence-to-actor-reasoning-to-forecast workflow complement your case-teaching tools (e.g., MobLab Classroom)?
What would you need to see before piloting this in an MBA or executive program?
cases.consent_requirement: Explicit verbal consent; no commercial claims without evidence; INSEAD IRB protocols apply if student data is involved. Separate quality-improvement from research per HHS OHRP guidance.
cases.stop_failure_rule: Stop if target declines consent or indicates no teaching need
cases.moblab_complement_note: This case positions the workflow as a complement to MobLab Classroom, not a replacement.

validation.cross_artifact_check: validated design-only mapping: EXP-007 education/H-EDU-4; EXP-008 enterprise/H-ENT-0; no execution, outreach, or hypothesis transition

approval_required_before_execution: Owner approval to execute EXP-007 and/or EXP-008

regulatory_references.education_privacy: U.S. Department of Education privacy guidance (https://studentprivacy.ed.gov/)
regulatory_references.research_vs_qi: HHS OHRP guidance on quality improvement vs research (https://www.hhs.gov/ohrp/regulations-and-policy/guidance/)
